title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: If you visit the US during the winter months, chances are you're going to encounter some cold weather.
So today, I am going to teach you some cold weather English.
That's not going to happen.
And there's a dollopie on the card, so I'm going to be 44.

title: will / shall
examples: Yeah, I'll just take the price tags off and then whenever you're ready to come back, just get my attention.
I will never register to participate in this event again.
We'll take care of you, cameraman.
Yeah, I'll just take the price tags off.
And I'll take a Pepsi.

title: When I do / When I've done
examples: It's just when you get some of that wind, then it gets bitter cold.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: And if you do want a hand carry and a mount, I can help you guys carry.
I can tell he's not used to Michigan winters like my mother and I.
And if you do want a hand carrying amount, I can help you guys carry it.
You could go with that.
Can I have a lemonade, too?

title: have to / must
examples: I'd have to have a lot of drinks before jumping in that water.
Yeah, we'll have to show you one to you.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: I'd have to have a lot of drinks before jumping in that water.
I would not expect to run into someone I know up here, since this is not my hometown.

title: If I do / If I did
examples: And if you do want a hand carry and a mount, I can help you guys carry.
And if you do want a hand carrying amount, I can help you guys carry it.

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: The first event we came to see is called the Polar Plunge.
My mom and I are dressed in layers, but the cameraman isn't even wearing boots.
Actually, that's why this festival is called Tip Up Town.
